personal.name: Francisco Saltiel
personal.title: Computer Science Student
personal.summary: Computer Science student with experience in software development, computer graphics, database design, undergraduate research, and leadership through Air Force ROTC. Interested in practical software systems, simulation, graphics, and interactive applications.

entries.title: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
entries.organization: University of Puerto Rico at Mayagüez
entries.description: Undergraduate student focused on computer science, software development, and applied computing projects.
bullets: Completed advanced coursework in algorithms, operating systems, computer graphics, databases, software engineering, and human-computer interaction.
Developed academic projects involving graphics programming, database normalization, web applications, and algorithm visualization.

entries.title: Cadet Development Supervisor
entries.description: Supervised cadet development and training throughout an academic semester.
bullets: Directly supervised the development of more than 20 cadets over the course of a full semester.
Provided guidance, feedback, and accountability to support cadet performance and leadership growth.
Coordinated expectations, preparation, and communication for recurring training activities.

entries.title: Deputy Commander, Cadet Training Event
entries.description: Served as the second-highest cadet leader for a large training event.
bullets: Helped lead a cadet training event involving approximately 90 cadets.
Supported event planning, command structure, and real-time decision-making.
Coordinated with other cadet leaders to support safe and organized event execution.

entries.title: Logistics Readiness Officer
entries.description: Oversaw material logistics for a large cadet organization.
bullets: Managed material logistics for a detachment of more than 100 cadets throughout an academic semester.
Tracked, organized, and coordinated equipment and resources needed for training activities.
Supported operational readiness by maintaining accountability of assigned materials.

entries.title: Undergraduate Research Student
entries.organization: DCT-Based Digital Image Watermarking Reproduction and Analysis
entries.location: University of Puerto Rico at Mayagüez
entries.description: Reproduced and analyzed a digital image watermarking method based on discrete cosine transform techniques.
bullets: Implemented a DCT-based watermarking system using Python, NumPy, OpenCV, and Matplotlib.
Evaluated watermark robustness using metrics such as PSNR, BER, and normalized correlation.
Tested performance under image attacks such as compression, noise, cropping, and rotation.

entries.title: CPU-Based Rasterizer with SSAO
entries.location: University of Puerto Rico at Mayagüez
entries.description: Built a CPU-based rasterizer from scratch and implemented screen space ambient occlusion.
bullets: Developed a rasterization pipeline in C++ without relying on a GPU rendering framework.
Implemented SSAO to improve depth and lighting perception in a test scene.
Created a custom scene to demonstrate and compare the visual effect of SSAO.

entries.title: Pathfinding Algorithm Visualizer
entries.location: University of Puerto Rico at Mayagüez
entries.description: Built an interactive visualizer comparing A*, Dijkstra's algorithm, and breadth-first search.
bullets: Created an interactive grid interface where users could draw mazes and place pathfinding points.
Displayed side-by-side comparisons of A*, Dijkstra's algorithm, and BFS.
Implemented weighted edges to support more detailed algorithm comparison and analysis.

entries.title: Programming Languages Midterm Website
entries.location: University of Puerto Rico at Mayagüez
entries.description: Built a multi-program website using different programming languages and paradigms.
bullets: Implemented browser-based programs using JavaScript, Python/PyScript, Prolog/Tau Prolog, Elm, and TypeScript.
Designed a consistent Bootstrap interface across multiple program pages.
Practiced front-end execution of imperative, functional, logical, and object-oriented programs.

entries.title: Project HERMES: Ares-Pathfinder Simulation
entries.location: University of Puerto Rico at Mayagüez
entries.description: Contributed to the planning and design of a terminal-based mission simulation prototype.
bullets: Defined role-based user tasks, system requirements, and prototype scope.
Supported incremental development planning and project documentation.
Focused on simulation design, requirements organization, and user interaction flow.

entries.title: Sales Database Normalization Project
entries.location: University of Puerto Rico at Mayagüez
entries.description: Converted sales sample data from a CSV file into a normalized relational database design using SQL.
bullets: Analyzed CSV-based sales data and identified entities, attributes, primary keys, and foreign key relationships.
Designed normalized relational tables to reduce redundancy and improve data organization.
Documented normalization decisions across multiple normal forms and represented relationships between entities.

entries.title: Discord Dungeon Crawler Game
entries.description: Developed a Discord-based dungeon crawler game prototype from scratch using Discord API interactions.
bullets: Implemented player stats, monster stats, experience progression, gear, inventory, shop systems, and a final boss encounter.
Created randomly generated dungeon progression and encounter systems.
Used event-driven programming and Discord API interactions to handle player commands and game responses.

entries.title: Discord Role Management Bot
entries.description: Created a Discord bot for server-wide role assignment and automation using Discord API interactions.
bullets: Built a bot that assigned and removed roles across a server of more than 200 members.
Practiced event-driven programming, permissions handling, role management, and server automation.
Used Discord API functionality to respond to commands and apply role changes across the server.

entries.title: Software Development
entries.description: Experience designing, implementing, debugging, and organizing programs across academic and personal projects.
bullets: Comfortable working with multi-file projects and breaking logic into separate components.
Experience building tools, visualizations, simulations, and browser-based applications.

entries.title: Web Development
entries.description: Experience with HTML, CSS, Bootstrap, JavaScript, and front-end program execution.
bullets: Built responsive pages using Bootstrap components and custom CSS.
Implemented user input, output rendering, validation, and dynamic page updates.

entries.title: Data and Databases
entries.description: Experience with SQL, SQLite, relational modeling, normalization, and structured data processing.
bullets: Designed relational schemas from semi-structured data.
Worked with JSON-style data, CSV datasets, and database normalization concepts.

entries.title: Graphics and Simulation
entries.description: Experience with C++ graphics programming, rasterization, SSAO, and simulation-oriented academic projects.
bullets: Implemented graphics techniques from scratch in an academic setting.
Worked with visual demonstrations and technical analysis of rendering effects.

entries.title: Primary Programming Experience
entries.description: Python and C++ are the programming languages used most extensively across coursework, research, and larger projects.
bullets: Python: research implementation, data processing, scripting, and browser execution through PyScript.
C++: academic programming experience, including computer graphics and rasterization work.

entries.title: Additional Programming Experience
entries.description: Additional experience through coursework and focused class projects.
bullets: Java: academic exposure through programming coursework.
JavaScript and TypeScript: front-end applications, DOM interaction, and object-oriented browser-based programming.
SQL: relational database design and querying.
Prolog and Elm: introductory experience through programming languages coursework.

entries.title: Development Tools
entries.description: VS Code, Git, GitHub, and command-line development workflows.
bullets: Used version control and local development tools to organize class and personal programming projects.

entries.title: Web and Browser Tools
entries.description: Bootstrap, PyScript, Tau Prolog, Elm compiler, TypeScript compiler, and browser developer tools.
bullets: Used browser-compatible tools to run multiple programming languages from a static website.

entries.title: Data and Research Tools
entries.description: SQLite, Google Colab, NumPy, OpenCV, and Matplotlib.
bullets: Used Python-based research and data tools to implement, test, and analyze image processing methods.
